text:
City Neurology Service

Outpatient Department

Clinic Date: 14 September 2025

Dr Susan
Riverside Health Centre
12 Brook Street, London W1F 7AL

Dear Dr Susan,

William Harris, DOB: 15-02-1965, Hospital No: H563829 NHS No. 9273645810
10 Meadow Park, London, EC1V 9BT

Summary/Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior head injury history; mild cognitive slowing; vitamin D insufficiency; hypertension; lives semi-independently with carer input; non-drinker; current smoker.

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.
Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily.
Cholecalciferol 800 IU once daily, Amlodipine 5 mg once daily, Omeprazole 20 mg once daily, Sertraline 50 mg once daily. ï¿½

I saw Bill in clinic today accompanied by his keyworker, Alex. He reports improved sleep and more regular meals since joining a day-activity programme. Energy levels are fair. On observation, gait is cautious but unaided; he can rise from a chair without using his arms. Tone is mildly increased on the right, with good passive range at the hip and knee; no focal tenderness. There is a small hard skin patch under the left forefoot that may contribute to occasional discomfort when walking long distances.

Seizures: Alex maintains a basic calendar log. According to both Bill and Alex, there have been no events of concern since his last review; in other words, there have been no episodes reported in the intervening period. The previous record available to us notes scattered brief spells in the earlier part of last year, but none documented of late. Ã©

Mood and cognition: Bill describes stable mood with intermittent word-finding pauses. He continues memory strategies (phone reminders). No recent alcohol; smoking reduced to five per day. â€

Examination: Alert, oriented, fluent speech with occasional hesitancy. Cranial nerves intact. Power full throughout; mild right-sided pyramidal pattern increased tone; coordination intact; gait steady, narrow-based. BP 128/76, pulse 68 regular. Weight stable. Ã‚Â·

Plan:
- Continue current antiseizure regimen unchanged.
- Podiatry referral for forefoot callosity and footwear advice.
- Vitamin D to continue; recheck U&E/LFT/Vit D at the surgery within the next month if not done in the past 6 months.
- Smoking cessation support signposted via local service; patient agreeable to referral.
- Routine follow-up in 6 months; earlier review if any new events are witnessed or documented.

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: there have been no events of concern since his last review; in other words, there have been no episodes reported in the intervening period.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states there have been no seizures since the last review: "there have been no events of concern since his last review; ... no episodes reported in the intervening period." The length of the intervening period is not specified, so I cannot confirm it is at least 6 months. Additional context notes no documented events "of late" and prior brief spells in the earlier part of last year, suggesting a period of seizure freedom but without a precise duration. Therefore, per the rules, classify as seizure free for multiple months.
seizure_frequency_number: there have been no events of concern since his last review; in other words, there have been no episodes reported in the intervening period.
